body_txt: While saving money and going for cheaper alternatives is a good idea in several circumstances, it can backfire almost as often. This trope deals with the situations where it does backfire. Depending on the work, this trope comes in two variants: in more comedic works, this is the result of a certain character being a cheapskate and deciding to go with a cheaper solution to his problem, which usually leads to Hilarity Ensuing. In more dramatic works, this is the result of a Corrupt Corporate Executive deciding to cut corners in areas where doing so leads to disastrous results (many times of the "loss of human life and or health" variety). If the situation involves a good or service that was received for free, this may involve some degree of Complaining About Things You Haven't Paid For . The bane of any Doom It Yourself enthusiast, especially when it comes to the dreaded Do-It-Yourself Plumbing Project . Cutting Corners is just another way of invoking this aesop. In video games, this trope is one of many reasons why Adam Smith Hates Your Guts .
